heading: Prohibition on former members of the armed forces accepting post-service employment with certain foreign governments
label: 989 Prohibition on former members of the armed forces accepting post-service employment with certain foreign governments
notes: Counterintelligence Briefings for Members of the Armed Forces, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–60, div. F, title LXV, § 6511139 Stat. 1629Dec. 18, 2025“(a).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—Covered individualThe term ‘covered individual’ has the meaning given such term in , United States Code.section 989(h) of title 10“(2).—Government of concern; company of concernThe terms ‘government of concern’ and ‘company of concern’ mean, respectively, a government described in subparagraph (A) of , United States Code, and a company, entity, or other person described in subparagraph (B) of such section.section 989(h)(2) of title 10“(b).—In GeneralThe Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Security shall issue appropriate policy to require the military departments to conduct counterintelligence briefings for members of the Armed Forces as part of the process required by , United States Code.section 989(c) of title 10“(c).—ElementsEach briefing provided under subsection (b) shall provide members of the Armed Forces with—“(1)awareness of methods commonly used by governments and companies of concern to solicit and learn from covered individuals sensitive military techniques, tactics, and procedures of the Armed Forces;“(2)recommended practices for covered individuals to avoid an activity that could subject such individuals to civil or criminal penalties;“(3)the contact information for the counterintelligence authorities to whom covered individuals should report attempted recruitment or a related suspicious contact; and“(4)an overview of the prohibition and penalties under subsections (a) and (c) of , United States Code.section 989 of title 10“(d).—Provision of Briefings at Certain TrainingsThe Under Secretary may mandate the briefings required by subsection (b) during the trainings required by Department of Defense Directive 5240.06 (relating to counterintelligence awareness and reporting), or successor document.”

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Except as provided by subsection (b), a covered individual may not occupy a covered post-service position.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual, or a Federal agency on behalf of, and with the consent of, the individual, submits to the Secretary a written application for a waiver in such form and manner as the Secretary determines appropriate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary determines that the waiver is necessary to advance the national security interests of the United States.

children.heading: .—Period of waiver
children.label: 2 .—Period of waiver
children.text: A waiver issued under paragraph (1) shall apply for a period not exceeding 5 years. The Secretary may renew such a waiver.

children.heading: .—Revocation
children.label: 3 .—Revocation
children.text: The Secretary may revoke a waiver issued under paragraph (1) to a covered individual with respect to a covered-post service position if the Secretary determines that the employment of the individual in the covered-post service position poses a threat to national security.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the Secretary issues a waiver under paragraph (1) or revokes a waiver under paragraph (3), the Secretary shall submit to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives written notification of the waiver or revocation, as the case may be.

children.label: I
children.text: the details of the application, including the position held by the individual in the armed forces;

children.label: II
children.text: the nature of the post-service position of the individual;

children.label: III
children.text: a description of the national security interests that will be advanced by reason of issuing such a waiver; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the specific reasons why the Secretary determines that issuing the waiver will advance such interests.

children.label: I
children.text: the details of the waiver, including any renewals of the waiver, and the dates of such waiver and renewals; and

children.label: II
children.text: the specific reasons why the Secretary determined that the revocation is warranted.
informed in writing of the prohibition, and the penalties for violations of the prohibition; and
required to certify that the member understands the prohibition and those penalties.
withhold any pay, allowances, or benefits that would otherwise be provided to the individual by the Department of Defense; and
revoke any security clearance of the individual.

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: 1 .—Requirement
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the congressional defense committees a report on covered post-service employment occurring during the year covered by the report.March 31, 2024

children.label: i
children.text: the name of the employer;

children.label: ii
children.text: the foreign government, including by the specific foreign individual, agency, or entity, for whom the covered post-service employment is being performed; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the nature of the services provided as part of the covered post-service employment.

children.label: i
children.text: the Department of Defense maintains adequate systems and processes for ensuring that former members of the armed forces are submitting required reports relating to their employment by foreign governments;

children.label: ii
children.text: all covered individuals who occupy a covered post-service position are in compliance with this section;

children.label: iii
children.text: the services provided by the covered individuals who occupy a covered post-service position pose a current or future threat to the national security of the United States; and

children.label: iv
children.text: there is any credible information or reporting that any covered individual who occupies a covered post-service position has engaged in activities that violate Federal law.

children.heading: .—Form of report
children.label: 3 .—Form of report
children.text: Each report required by paragraph (1) shall be submitted in unclassified form, but may include a classified annex.

children.heading: Notifications of Determinations of Certain Threats.—
children.label: f Notifications of Determinations of Certain Threats.—

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: 1 .—Requirement
children.text: In addition to the annual reports under subsection (d), if the Secretary determines that the services provided by a covered individual who occupies a covered post-service position pose a threat described in clause (iii) of paragraph (2)(B) of that subsection, or include activities described in clause (iv) of such paragraph, the Secretary shall notify the congressional defense committees of that determination by not later than 30 days after making the determination.

children.label: A
children.text: The name of the covered individual.

children.label: B
children.text: The name of the employer.

children.label: C
children.text: The foreign government, including the specific foreign individual, agency, or entity, for whom the covered post-service employment is being performed.

children.label: D
children.text: As applicable, a description of the risk to national security and the activities that may violate Federal law.

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: g .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to indemnify or shield covered individuals from prosecution under any relevant provision of title 18.

children.heading: .—Covered individual
children.label: 1 .—Covered individual
children.text: The term “covered individual” means an individual who has retired or otherwise separated from an active or reserve component of the Armed Forces.

children.label: i
children.text: a country of concern (as defined in section 1(m) of the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 ()); or22 U.S.C. 2651a(m)

children.label: ii
children.text: a country the Secretary of Defense determines acts as a proxy or passthrough for services for a country of concern; or

children.label: B
children.text: any company, entity, or other person the activities of which are directly or indirectly supervised, directed, controlled, financed, or subsidized, in whole or in major part, by a government described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Covered post-service position
children.label: 3 .—Covered post-service position
children.text: The term “covered post-service position” means a position of employment described in paragraph (2).
